title: Verify browser authenticated wishlist flow
verify: Real browser authenticated session adds from catalog/detail, shows on wishlist/reload, and removes across all views.
Second customer sees no first-customer favorite and cannot affect it; guest click routes to login without persistence.
Missing/unpublished/channel-invisible/inactive-category products are omitted, restored products reappear, and out-of-stock remains visible/unavailable.
Wishlist remains usable with a valid current customer during merge_blocked while checkout remains blocked; logout/session expiry clears UI state and browser storage.
evidence_required: Playwright trace/report for add/view/remove/reload across catalog/detail/wishlist; synthetic product IDs/names and synthetic wishlist contents are allowed as visual proof.
Two-customer, guest non-persistence, and merge_blocked-independence assertions.
Product visibility/out-of-stock and logout/session-expiry assertions plus scan excluding real PII, production data, cookies, bearer values, OAuth tokens, session IDs, and secrets.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use real browser, storefront, Medusa, Wishlist Module, PostgreSQL, and synthetic authenticated sessions/content.
Use existing FT-004 production session boundary or its existing local harness; add no production bearer mechanism.
Synthetic wishlist content may appear in acceptance artifacts; real PII, production data, cookies, bearer values, OAuth tokens, session IDs, and secrets may not.
invariants: Customer isolation remains observable across separate sessions.
Valid current-customer capability permits wishlist during merge_blocked while checkout remains blocked.
Browser state clears on logout/session expiry while backend durable ownership remains correct.
purpose: Prove the complete buyer-visible authenticated wishlist in a real browser without live providers or production data.
success_outcome: Catalog/detail/wishlist UI preserves durable customer-isolated favorites, handles product/session changes, and creates no guest/browser persistence.
anti_goals: Do not use real PII, production data, credentials, tokens, or session identifiers.
Do not alter production wishlist/auth/catalog behavior from acceptance code.
Do not add variant favorites, sharing, or recommendations.

stop_conditions: E2E cannot exercise real storefront/Medusa/PostgreSQL boundaries.
Auth harness bypasses standard Medusa ownership semantics or requires a new production bearer path.
Evidence would require real PII, production data, cookies, bearer values, OAuth tokens, session IDs, or secrets.
